teamObjective.explanation: With a very weak hand of only 3-6 non-trump spades, I should lead the lowest card to minimize resource loss and give my partner the cheapest possible chance to take control of the trick.

partnerBelief.summary: My partner has not acted, so I have no direct signal; they likely hold some higher non-trump cards or trumps that could beat a low spade lead, but this is probabilistic.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponents likely hold some trumps or higher cards since my hand is weak, but no public play has revealed their strength.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the 3 of spades expends the minimum useful card, preserves the slightly higher spades for later, and invites my partner to win the trick cheaply without forcing me to commit a better card.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: It spends my highest card immediately; if an opponent or partner beats it, I lose my best chance to influence a later trick.
Better than the 6 but still wastes a middling card when the 3 can probe the table for free.
Only marginally better than the 5 or 6; the 3 is still strictly cheaper and carries the same informational value.

risks: An opponent may play a trump or higher card and seize the lead.
My partner may be unable to beat whatever follows, leaving us without control.
riskAssessment.mitigation: By leading the very lowest card, I limit the value I sacrifice if the trick is lost and keep my slightly higher cards for future exchanges.
